description: Tasty and wonderfully drinkable. The basic flavors are cola and soy, with extra nuances of blackberries, cherries and quite a bit of smoky, sweet oak. Drinks very dry and spicy.
title: Savannah-Chanelle 2000 Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: It's too hot for Pinot Noir in Paso Robles, right? This wine shows why. It's not bad, but clumsy, with heavy, earthy flavors. It lacks delicacy and subtlety, which is what Pinot Noir is all about.
title: Zenaida Cellars 2000 Pinot Noir (Paso Robles)

description: A deep gold color sends up warning flags, and the flavors bear that out. Has simple apple flavors with a touch of shellac and no depth. Kosher.
title: Bartenura 2000 Pinot Grigio (Veneto)

description: This full-bodied effort goes down surprisingly easy. Aromas of shoe polish, black plums and a dash of black pepper presage flavors of blackberry, plum and brandied spice cake. Finishes with warm notes of chocolate and spice. —J.C.
title: Kangarilla Road 2000 Shiraz (McLaren Vale)

description: This is the first vintage of this wine that will be available in the United States and it makes a positive first impression. It's a delicious blend of spice, floral notes and strawberries that finishes with soft, caressing tannins.
title: Marchesi de' Frescobaldi 2000 Campo ai Sasso  (Rosso di Montalcino)

description: This structured, medium-weight wine's nose offers dried apple and nutty aromas. Crisp lime and stony notes come up strongly on the palate. The mouth shows good tension and decent acidity, the flavors are persistent on the solid finish of this tasty white. Drink now–2004. Best Buy. —M.M.
title: Huguet de Can Feixes 2000 Can Feixes Blanc Selecciò White (Catalonia)

description: This Livermore producer has sourced from Livermore and Amador County—both Portuguese varieties and Cabernet Sauvignon—to make this Port-style wine, a mouthful of caramel toffee melted and toasty. A hint of marshmallow's there too.
title: Cedar Mountain 2000 Late Bottled Vintage Port Port (California)

description: This amber-colored Marsala opens with earthy tones of porcini mushroom, caramel, burnt sugar and candied fruit. It's a thick, syrupy wine with savory, leathery details on the long finish.
title: Cantine Florio 2000 Terre Arse Vergine  (Marsala)

description: Mustard-seed aromas pierce the nose, which otherwise is mostly apple and pineapple. Lemon, green apple and passion fruit flavors are accented by the lightest touch of oregano and basil. For a cru, it's rather simple, with extremely light fruit on the finish and very little length or substance to the back end.
title: Pascal Jolivet 2000 Chateau du Nozay  (Sancerre)

description: Grapefruit mixed with honey and some green veggies welcome you in, while the intriguing palate provides citrus, more honey, apricots and even some scallions. Simple and straightforward it's not; but it is fairly easy to drink. Served chilled on the front porch during the summer.
title: Voss 2000 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Talk about an assorted but attractive bouquet: this has lemon, peach, dried herbs—even some honey. The flavors veer mostly toward citrus—predominantly lemon and orange. But there's a touch of sweet pineapple in there, too. The finish is spicy and maintains the citrus from the palate, but it does tail off a bit heavy and hot.
title: EOS 2000 Reserve Fumé Blanc (Paso Robles)

description: Nickolaihof produces intense, full-flavored Rieslings that age more quickly than Wachau Rieslings from further west. This is a silky, creamy wine, full of white fruit flavors, seared through with crisp acidity, enormously concentrated, with a bone-dry finish.
title: Nikolaihof 2000 Im Weingebirge Smaragd Riesling (Wachau)

description: A finely structured, delicate, elegant wine, this has a pure white fruit and mineral character that lies easily on the palate. The aftertaste is soft but there is still a steely edge to the wine, which promises long aging. Give it at least 5 years.
title: Louis Roederer 2000 Blanc de Blancs Brut Chardonnay (Champagne)

description: A round, ripe pear and lemon nose opens this medium-weight, full flavored white. Grapefruit, spice and green apple coat the palate, the texture is fairly rich—maybe even fat for Riesling—but there's good acidity to support the fruit. Finishes long with white peach and mineral notes.
title: Penfolds 2000 Reserve Riesling (Eden Valley)

description: This is a wonderful, dense wine, very concentrated, very intensely flavored. At this stage of its development, it is the opulence which is most evident, but there are already hints that it will show pure fruit alongside sweet tannins, and will develop complexity.
title: Château la Fleur-Pétrus 2000  Pomerol

description: A lush, rich  Port with a smooth texture and flavors that range from black cherry, blackberry, cassis, cinnamon, anise, chocolate, toffee, butterscotch and thyme. It's complex yet delightfully easy to drink.
title: Pessagno 2000 Hames Valley Vineyard Port (Monterey)

description: A light, smooth quaffer that'll get you through the summer, this blend of 71% Shiraz and 29% Cabernet Sauvignon boasts aromas and flavors of charcoal-grilled black cherries tinged with chocolate. —J.C.
title: Jacob's Creek 2000 Shiraz-Cabernet Sauvignon (South Eastern Australia)

description: Flavorful, forward and tasting of a pleasing mix of light, toasty oak and pretty cherry fruit, this is a solid, immediately enjoyable wine. It doesn't have the muscle to go long term, but it offers a lot of pure Merlot pleasure in the near term. —P.G.
title: Woodward Canyon 2000 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Butter, pain grillé and roasted nut aromas expand on the palate into honey, peach and tangerine flavors, accented by citrus and mineral notes. Spicy (white peppery) on the finish.
title: Louis Latour 2000 Morgeot  (Chassagne-Montrachet)

description: In time, this may surpass the regular bottling, but for now it trails by a nose, thanks to some mushroom, bracken and lima bean scents that dampen the cola and beet flavors. As always, it's big, rich and full-bodied. Try in 2005.
title: Felton Road 2000 Block 3 Pinot Noir (Central Otago)

description: About 15,000 cases were made of this simple, stainless-fermented, very clean and fruity wine. Nice cinnamon spice and light hazelnut flavors light up the finish. A lot of flavor and style for this price.
title: Bridgeview 2000 Chardonnay (Oregon)

description: Winemaker Doug Meador says this is not a “California Fats” Chardonnay, by which he means he went light on the oak and allowed the fruit to speak for itself. It's pretty fruit, with the smell and taste of apples. However, that slight touch of oak adds just the right amount of vanilla and cream to the mix. —S.H.
title: Ventana 2000 Gold Stripe Chardonnay (Arroyo Seco)

description: This balanced wine has a nose of vanilla, cardamom that lingers on to the mouthful of simple, oaky red fruit. There is an herby note of radicchio that clings from the nose to the cinnamon toast finish.
title: Chamonix 2000 Troika Red (Franschhoek)

description: A Julia Roberts of a Merlot, pretty and innocent, maybe a little clueless. But who cares? It's fruity, dry, and fun, and even has some layers of complexity. Best of all, it doesn't cost too much. Drink up!
title: ForestVille 2000 Sonoma Reserve Merlot (Alexander Valley)

description: Apricot aromas with flavors of green apple, cucumber and green bean. The finish is clean and easy, but bland. This wine does nothing to offend, but it doesn't do much to impress, either.
title: Morandé 2000 Pionero Sauvignon Blanc (Central Valley)

description: Complex and intriguing in the nose, the wine is weighty on the palate and shows a fine blend of citrus, herb, melon, toast, pear and spice flavors. It finishes long and clean.
title: Chaddsford 2000 Philip Roth Vineyard Chardonnay (Pennsylvania)

description: White stone fruits, some smoke and toast, and ample spice makes the entrance to this wine really inviting. There's plenty of richness to the mouth, which is jammed with lemon, lime and a balancing dryness not unlike chalk. The fade is pure and dry, offering long nuances of spicy oak and white pepper. It's a wine that's integrated and satisfying.
title: Benziger 2000 Estate Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Mountain)

description: Round and sweet, with some grapy, creamy aromas and a hint of oak sticking out. The palate is sweet, a bit candied, and firm, all courtesy of sharp but healthy tannins. A sweet, chewy finish is the lasting impression.
title: Morandé 2000 House of Morande Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Pieropan's La Rocca distinguishes itself from the Calvarino bottling by firmer structure and greater ageability. Tasted in spring 2003, this effort still has plenty of life left. Mimics Sauvignon a little in its gooseberry and red-currant flavors and grapefruity acidity.
title: Pieropan 2000 La Rocca  (Soave Classico Superiore)

description: Heavier and denser in every respect than the regular Sauvignon Blanc, this wine, a 60-40 blend with Sémillon, is also strongly leesy. The citrus flavors hit the palate with a strong dose of rich sour cream notes. The wine is dry and practically cries out for garlicky dishes.
title: Buttonwood Farm 2000 Devin Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: In contrast to many of the other wines, this one starts off tight, with only faint lime and mineral notes on the nose. But it blossoms with air, stubbornly yielding up taut citrus flavors that flow over the palate with an almost gritty, powdered-rock texture.
title: Louis Latour 2000 Chateau De Blagny Premier Cru  (Meursault)

description: Boasts plenty of buttery tropical fruit set off by aromas of toast and smoke, then shines in the mouth, where the mouthfeel is custardy but not heavy and the fruit flavors shine. Finishes long and tart, seamlessly turning citrusy along the way.
title: Xanadu 2000 Chardonnay (Margaret River)

description: A round, solidly made wine, with Bing cherry flavors. However, the middle seems to disappear and, as a result, the following tannins feel a bit harsh. —P.G.
title: Sagelands 2000 Four Corners Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: From R.H. Phillips, itÕs a shade oakier and more refined than their regular bottling. The peach flavors are more concentrated, and sappier, but it comes at the cost of crispness. The wine sags in the mouth, like a Dali melted watch. But it tastes good.
title: R.H. Phillips 2000 Toasted head Chardonnay (Dunnigan Hills)

description: Actually a Bordeaux blend, this mainly merlot wine from the Umpqua valley in central Oregon outdoes its peers with dark, plummy, even jammy fruit laced with toast, tar and espresso. Sweet fruit meets stiff tannins, all in the name of structure.
title: Abacela 2000 Merlot (Umpqua Valley)

description: Tasty fresh fruit, leaning toward grapefruit and pineapple, peach and lemon. Zesty and forward, it refreshes rather than fatigues the palate, and is perfect for sipping on a spring afternoon. Best Buy.
title: Snoqualmie 2000 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The millennium year, 2000, was not the greatest Champagne vintage. However, Boizel has released a prestige cuvée from that year and produced a well matured wine with a dry character and plenty of toasty notes. Dominated by Pinot Noir, it is deliciously rich and totally ready to drink.
title: Boizel 2000 Joyau de France Brut  (Champagne)

description: Toasty and oaky, but in a warm and lovable way, all underpinned by lean citrusy fruit that helps maintain a sense of balance and proportion. There's even a bit of crème brûlée thrown into the mix. Finishes smoothly, with nary a rough edge. Best Buy.
title: McPherson Wine Company 2000 Murray-Darling Chardonnay (Australia)

description: A good wine. It's nicely dry, with crisp acids and adequate tannins. There's a pretty good balance between plum and berry flavors and earthy, herbal ones. It's rough around the edges, with a burnt-rubber quality, but okay drinking for everyday occasions. —S.H.
title: Coastal Ridge 2000 Shiraz (California)

description: Here's a simple little wine that makes for pleasant drinking at an affordable price. It's lemony and dry and soft in acids, but it is very clean and is fine for fried chicken, fruit salad and other picnic-type fare. —S.H.
title: Stonehedge 2000 Sauvignon Blanc (California)

description: Vegetal and herbal, with disageeable aromas of canned white asparagus. Tastes a little better than it smells, but hardly like a Chardonnay. Flavors are citrusy, and the wine is lean and tart.
title: Bridlewood 2000 Chardonnay (Edna Valley)

description: Smooth and round from the start, with aromas of plum, cassis and cola. In the mouth, there's cherry and more cassis, and as a whole it's downright tasty. On the back side, the finish is firm and chocolaty, with good tannins. After tasting many a mediocre Pinot Noir from this Eugene-based winery, this flavorful if one-dimensional Cabernet is a revelation. Only 150 cases produced.
title: Silvan Ridge 2000 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rogue Valley)

description: Like you might expect from a maturing gran reserva, Imaz offers glimpses of raisin, sweet berry and even some aged cheese. In the mouth, it's all about red fruit, although an earthy undertone provides some background. Feels a little stark, zesty and lean, while the finish is solid and tastes of vanilla and leather. Drink now through 2012.
title: El Coto 2000 Coto de Imaz Gran Reserva  (Rioja)

description: A tart, chalky finish suggests a couple years of cellaring would help this flavorful wine blossom because the other ingredients are already in place: Black pepper and eucalyptus   add complexity to the blackberry aromas and flavors. —J.C.
title: Warrabilla 2000 Shiraz (Rutherglen)

description: Gorgeous color and concentration with brilliant ruby highlights make this a beautiful wine to contemplate in the glass. The aromas are broad and far-reaching with a high mineral component, small berry fruit, spice and a distinct soapy-lavender element. Chewy, firm and succulent, it's a mouth-watering bruiser that leaves a lasting imprint on the palate. Only 60 cases made.
title: Vaona 2000 Pegrandi  (Amarone della Valpolicella Classico)

description: Starts off with mineral, lemon and anise aromas that develop on the palate into dried spices, ground flint and lime. It's light in body, yet finishes strong, with clean citrus flavors and a lasting sensation of powdery talc.
title: Stag's Leap Wine Cellars 2000 Rancho Chimiles Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Soft and jammy, with black and red wild berry flavors and low acidity. ItÕs so soft and sweetly fruity itÕs almost like raspberry syrup, although it does have some structure and heat from alcohol. Fans of sweet wines will like it a lot.
title: Coulson Eldorado 2000 Kipp Vineyard Mataro (El Dorado)

description: The red plum and cassis aromas are meaty and oak-laden and carry to the smoky palate. This simple wine shows firm tannins and good acids, finishing with a toasty herbal note.
title: Meinert 2000 Cabernet Sauvignon (Devon Valley)

description: The charming nose of freshly picked berries promises good things to come. Off-dry, but not cloying, the palate is approachable and flirty, with fresh fruit flavors and a crisp finish. Not terribly complex, but overall a good go-to sparkling wine.
title: Château Frank 2000 Blanc de Noirs  (Finger Lakes)

description: Very fragrant, with aromas of violets, charred meat and ample black fruit. The flavor profile is uncommon but appealing, surely a result of the blend, which is Cabernet Sauvignon along with Carmenère and Petit Verdot. On the finish, you get a hint of charcoal in addition to espresso. If it were deeper and more complex it could potentially be a great wine.
title: Casa Silva 2000 Quinta Generacion Red (Colchagua Valley)

description: Made of equal parts dried Trebbiano and Malvasia grapes, this dessert wine has intense toasted almond, maple syrup and caramel aromas. The dense, structured palate delivers acacia honey, dried fig and toasted hazelnut flavors.
title: Losi Querciavalle 2000  Vin Santo del Chianti Classico

description: This medium-bodied Carmenère offers blackberry, oak and white-pepper flavors and herb, wood and blueberry aromas. Good for a group; will appeal to a wide audience.
title: Portal Del Alto 2000 Gran Reserva Carmenère (Maule Valley)

description: Quite spicy, with cinnamon and peppery cherry, raspberry, black currant, anise, tea and plum notes. Smooth, silky tannins frame it all, with a mellow finish. Quite nice. Editors' Choice.
title: Williams Selyem 2000 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)

description: Defines the old style of Carneros Pinot, with juicy flavors of raspberries and cherries and the silkiest, smoothest tannins you ever had. Very dry, with the tart, peppery acidity that makes it a natural partner for food. Yet it's fundamentally a light wine with limited aging potential.
title: Mayo 2000 Sangiacomo Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)

description: A plump but graceful wine, brimming with sour plum and cherry flavors. Finishes tart and lemony, but with coffee and chocolate accents as well. It's slow out of the blocks, so give it a brisk decanting prior to serving.
title: Michaud 2000 Pinot Noir (Chalone)

description: From a very warm site in pear country comes this Cabernet/Merlot blend. It shows ripe fruit, dense tannin, and some dark, toasted-coffee notes. The forward fruit is appealing, but the wine falls off in the middle and finishes quickly.
title: Siskiyou Vineyards 2000 La Cave Rouge Red (Oregon)

description: A mere 40 cases of this LD exists, a lucky find in the cellar laid down in 2001 and forgotten about until this year. A blend of 60% Pinot Noir and 40% Chardonnay, it's fragrant in dried fruit and boasts a thick, unctuous mouthfeel. Briny, dry and tart, with the taste of fresh-squeezed lemon, it's a philosophical exercise in the art of patience.
title: Iron Horse 2000 Brut LD Sparkling (Green Valley)
